description: A generation: the people moving through life together, and the interval between parents and children. The word covers both, as most languages let it: 'mia noruwa' (my generation), 'wi noruwa' (two generations).
sound_symbolism: 'no' grounds it in people, 'ru' rolls it forward, 'wa' opens to the ones after. The word moves in one direction, as its meaning does.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'theula noruwa melira meliho.' (Every generation sings the songs.)

pillars.solarpunk-values: Seventh-generation thinking needs the word: decisions weighed against people who do not exist yet, counted in noruwa.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Where nothing was written, a generation was the unit of memory: what the old taught the young was the whole library.
